text.title: claude-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for claude-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: An agent without a MAX_TURNS guard calls tools indefinitely. Claude never reaches end_turn when a tool always returns an error, an ambiguous result, or a prompt that drives Claude to call more tools.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unbounded loops burn tokens at tool_use call rates plus tool result input rates. At Sonnet pricing a 1,000-turn runaway costs ~$5 before hitting rate limits.

failure-mode.description: A tool's input_schema is updated without updating the agent loop handler. Claude generates inputs matching the new schema; the handler expects the old field names and silently ignores or crashes on unknown fields.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema validation is not automatic — the API passes Claude's tool inputs directly without checking them against the registered schema on the server side.

failure-mode.description: An agentic loop executes irreversible side effects (database mutations, email sends, API POST calls) without a human confirmation step. Claude cannot undo executed side effects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Claude may misinterpret ambiguous instructions or act on stale context, triggering writes the user did not intend.

failure-mode.description: Prompt Caching TTL is 5 minutes. Long gaps between requests invalidate the cache. Applications with fewer than ~12 requests per hour may never benefit from caching.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cache TTL is fixed and not configurable. Low-traffic services pay cache write overhead on every cold request.

failure-mode.description: A cached block is prepended with a dynamic prefix (timestamp, request ID, user name) that changes between requests, invalidating the cache on every call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cache matching is prefix-based. Any content before the cached block that changes between requests breaks the prefix.

failure-mode.description: Streaming handler only listens for text_delta events. thinking blocks arrive before text blocks as thinking_delta events. The handler discards thinking tokens while still paying for them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Extended Thinking tokens are billed at the full model input rate. Ignoring them in the handler does not skip their billing.

failure-mode.description: Extended Thinking is enabled for a task that requires a short answer (under 200 tokens). The minimum 1,024-token budget makes every call expensive regardless of response length.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Budget tokens are always billed even if the model does not use the full budget.

failure-mode.description: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is hardcoded in source code or committed to version control. Key exposure leads to unauthorized usage and billing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: API keys in code are frequently leaked via git history, container images, or CI logs.

failure-mode.description: Agent makes parallel requests without exponential backoff. HTTP 429 responses crash the loop instead of being retried.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default tier rate limits (requests/minute, tokens/minute) are hit quickly in parallel agentic loops.
